# 3New SRL — EU MDR Consulting for Japanese Medical Device Manufacturers

> 3New SRL is a Belgian-based consultancy helping Japanese medical device manufacturers navigate EU MDR (Medical Device Regulation (EU) 2017/745) compliance, clinical evaluation, European market entry, and AI-assisted regulatory workflows. Services are provided in Japanese and English. Initial consultations are free.

## Key Facts

- Location: Belgium (European Union)
- Languages: Japanese (primary), English (available)
- Registered as Authorised Representative (AR) in EUDAMED
- Representative holds a Japanese patent attorney qualification
- Target clients: Japanese medical device manufacturers, startups, SMEs, and early-stage companies preparing for EU market entry

## Services

- [CER & Clinical Evaluation / Literature Search Support](https://3new.eu/cer-literature-search/): Support for Clinical Evaluation Reports (CER) under EU MDR. Literature search (PubMed, EMBASE), SOTA analysis, equivalent device assessment, CER drafting. MDCG 2020-1 and MDCG 2020-13 compliant.
- [Authorised Representative (AR)](https://3new.eu/authorised-representative/): AR selection, evaluation, contract review, mandate, QMS/SOP alignment. 3New SRL is registered as AR in EUDAMED. AR appointment subject to individual assessment.
- [Legal Representative & CRO Management](https://3new.eu/legal-representative-cro/): Legal Representative arrangements and CRO selection, management, and contract review for European clinical investigations.
- [Economic Operator Audit](https://3new.eu/economic-operator-audit/): MDR compliance gap analysis for AR, Importer, and Distributor obligations under Articles 11, 13, and 14 MDR.
- [GDPR & Belgium Market Access](https://3new.eu/gdpr-belgium-market-access/): GDPR compliance for clinical and PMS data, DPA review, Belgium entity setup, European market entry roadmap.
- [AI-Assisted EU MDR Workflows](https://3new.eu/ai-assisted-eu-mdr-workflows/): Task-specific AI workflow design for EU MDR activities. Covers literature search AI assist, CER review support, MDR/MDCG research, anonymization workflows, internal checklists and SOPs, and optional European expert review coordination. AI is positioned as a support tool, not a replacement for regulatory judgment.

## Resources

- [Column / Articles (Japanese)](https://3new.eu/column-note/): Free articles in Japanese on EU MDR practice, CER, AR, PRRC, GDPR, MDCG guidance, and AI-assisted MDR workflows.
- [Paid Templates & Checklists](https://3new.eu/templates-checklists/): Practical templates and checklists for EU MDR compliance work (in preparation). Includes CER checklists, literature search templates, AR audit checklists, GDPR checklists, AI workflow templates.
- [Paid Documents](https://3new.eu/products/): EU MDR practice documents including literature search guides, AR audit checklists, GDPR clinical investigation checklists.

## Company Information

- [About 3New SRL](https://3new.eu/about/): Company profile, representative background, EUDAMED registration details.
- [Contact](https://3new.eu/contact/): Initial consultations are free. Services in Japanese and English.
- [Privacy Policy](https://3new.eu/privacy-policy/): GDPR-compliant. Data processors: Stripe (payments), Brevo (newsletter, EU-based).
- [Disclaimer & COI Policy](https://3new.eu/disclaimer-coi-confidentiality/): Scope of services, conflict of interest policy, confidentiality policy.

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Is the initial consultation free?
A: Yes. Initial overview consultations (online, approximately 30-60 minutes) are free. Engagements involving technical document review, regulatory assessment, contract review, or analysis are charged.

Q: Do you support small manufacturers and startups?
A: Yes. 3New SRL welcomes enquiries from startups, early-stage companies, and SMEs regardless of company size or product stage.

Q: Is English available?
A: Yes. Services are available in Japanese and English. Coordination with European partners is conducted in English.

Q: How long does CER preparation take?
A: Typically 2-4 months from literature search to completed CER, depending on device complexity, number of relevant publications, and availability of existing documentation.

Q: What is an Authorised Representative (AR) under EU MDR?
A: Under Article 11 MDR, manufacturers outside the EU must designate an AR established in the EU. The AR acts as the manufacturer's EU contact for regulatory authorities, maintains copies of the Declaration of Conformity and technical documentation, and supports MDR compliance obligations.

Q: Can AI be used for EU MDR regulatory work?
A: AI can support document review, literature screening, summarisation, and checklist-based preparation. However, final regulatory judgment, clinical evaluation conclusions, benefit-risk decisions, and QMS approvals must remain with qualified human reviewers. 3New SRL designs controlled AI-assisted workflows that define where AI supports the process and where human review is required.

## Topics Covered

EU MDR, Medical Device Regulation (EU) 2017/745, CER, Clinical Evaluation Report, MDCG 2020-1, MDCG 2020-13, MDCG 2020-5, MDCG 2020-6, literature search, PubMed, EMBASE, SOTA, equivalent device, Notified Body, Authorised Representative, AR, PRRC, Person Responsible for Regulatory Compliance, Importer, Distributor, Economic Operator, Article 11 MDR, Article 13 MDR, Article 14 MDR, EUDAMED, UDI, GDPR, DPA, DPIA, clinical investigation, Legal Representative, CRO, PMS, PMCF, AI workflow, anonymization, human review, Japanese medical device manufacturer, EU market entry, Belgium
